content:
🚀 spritefusion-pixel-snapper | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Cleaning and standardizing AI-generated or procedurally created pixel art to fit a strict grid and color palette, making it suitable for game development and scalable assets.
✨ Key Features: • Snaps pixels to a perfect grid • Ensures consistent grid resolution • Quantizes colors to a strict palette • Preserves details like dithering
📖 Summary: Sprite Fusion Pixel Snapper is a Rust-based tool designed to clean up messy pixel art, particularly that generated by AI, by snapping pixels to a perfect grid and quantizing colors. It ensures consistency in pixel size and position, making art suitable for game development and scalable textures.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 DnsServer | C#
🎯 Primary Use Case: Self-hosting a private and secure DNS server to block ads, malware, and gain network insights.
✨ Key Features: • Self-hostable DNS server for privacy and security • Blocks ads and malware at DNS level • Supports DNS-over-TLS, DNS-over-HTTPS, and DNS-over-QUIC • User-friendly web console • Works out-of-the-box with minimal configuration
📖 Summary: Technitium DNS Server is an open-source, self-hostable DNS server that enhances privacy and security by blocking ads and malware at the DNS level. It offers advanced features like DNS-over-TLS, DNS-over-HTTPS, and DNS-over-QUIC, and provides network insights through logs and statistics.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 facex | C
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling real-time, on-device face analysis and recognition directly in web applications without server infrastructure.
✨ Key Features: • Runs entirely in the browser via WebAssembly • Includes face detection, 576-point 3D mesh, recognition, and anti-spoofing • Zero server-side dependencies • Trained from scratch with custom models • Encrypted weights (AES-256-GCM) for browser security
📖 Summary: FaceX is a comprehensive face processing pipeline that runs entirely within the browser using WebAssembly. It offers face detection, 576-point 3D mesh generation, recognition, and anti-spoofing capabilities without requiring any server-side processing or dependencies.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ Continuum | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: To enable persistent, collaborative AI coding agent workflows by providing a shared, live codebase context and inter-agent memory.
✨ Key Features: • Live code graph with tree-sitter parsing for multiple languages • Hybrid code search combining lexical (BM25) and semantic (model2vec) search • Code navigation features (definitions, callers, dependency graphs)
📖 Summary: Continuum is a persistent, multi-agent Model Context Protocol (MCP) server designed to provide AI coding agents with a shared, live view of a codebase and a long-term memory. It allows multiple agents to collaborate on a project, maintaining context and state across sessions and agents.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 portabase | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Simplifying and automating the backup and restoration of multiple database types for self-hosted environments.
✨ Key Features: • Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, MsSQL, MariaDB, Firebird SQL, SQLite, MongoDB, Redis, and Valkey • Automated CLI, Docker, Docker Compose, and Kubernetes (Helm) installation • Integrates with Portabase agents for secure operations • Self-hosted and open-source • Provides a dashboard for managing backups
📖 Summary: Portabase is a self-hosted, open-source tool designed to simplify database backup and restoration across a wide range of popular database systems. It leverages secure agents for operations and offers multiple installation methods including CLI, Docker, and Kubernetes.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 telegram-autopilot | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automating responses to Telegram messages using an AI assistant, allowing users to delegate routine communication.
✨ Key Features: • Responds in user's style via system prompt • One-button On/Off control • Two types of notes (contact and AI-generated) • Smart context awareness (sender, history, contact info) • Tone detection and adaptation
📖 Summary: Telegram-autopilot is a personal AI assistant that integrates with your Telegram profile to automatically respond to messages on your behalf. It leverages LLMs and Telegram's official Business Mode to handle routine communication, allowing users to maintain their personal style and context.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
